acceptance_criteria:
1. is_true() method implemented
2. Null -> false
3. Empty string -> false
4. Zero int/float -> false
5. NaN -> false
6. Empty containers -> false
7. Media -> true
8. Unit tests for truthiness edge cases
9. cargo test passes
description:
Implement the is_true() method for JinjaPromptValue - determines truthiness for {% if value %} conditions.

Location: crates/baml-bridge/src/prompt/jinja.rs

```rust
impl Object for JinjaPromptValue {
    fn is_true(self: &Arc<Self>) -> bool {
        // Value-based truthiness
        match &self.pv.value {
            BamlValue::Null => false,
            BamlValue::Bool(b) => *b,
            BamlValue::String(s) => !s.is_empty(),
            BamlValue::Int(i) => *i != 0,
            BamlValue::Float(f) => *f != 0.0 && !f.is_nan(),  // NaN is falsy
            BamlValue::List(items) => !items.is_empty(),
            BamlValue::Map(map) => !map.is_empty(),
            BamlValue::Class(_, fields) => !fields.is_empty(),
            BamlValue::Enum(_, variant) => !variant.is_empty(),
            BamlValue::Media(_) => true,  // Media is always truthy
        }
    }
}
```

Design decisions:
- NaN is falsy (avoids surprises with float edge cases)
- Empty containers are falsy
- Media is always truthy (it exists)
- Value wins over type (if type says List but value is null, it's falsy)
title: Implement JinjaPromptValue is_true for truthiness
